description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated, draped human figure rendered in a classical style, with detailed folds in the garment cascading over the legs and body. The figure is headless and armless, with the torso and lower body intact, suggesting it may be a fragment of a larger work or an ancient statue. The drapery is intricately carved, creating a sense of movement and depth, especially around the knees and lap. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with smoother surfaces and less pronounced folds, indicating the primary viewing angle is from the front. The material appears to be a pale, slightly weathered stone, possibly marble, with subtle variations in color and surface texture. The overall form is solid and heavy, with a stable base that allows it to sit upright.
description: A headless, armless seated human figure in classical draped attire, intricately carved from pale stone with detailed garment folds and a solid, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed drapery over the lap and chest, with the absence of head and arms clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep folds of the garment and the profile of the seated posture.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smoother, less detailed back and the broad expanse of the garment.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the curvature of the back and the way the drapery falls over the side.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
